evals.prompt: Create a NestJS products module with full CRUD. Include: ProductController, ProductService, CreateProductDto and UpdateProductDto with class-validator, Prisma for database access, and unit tests for the service. Follow NestJS module architecture best practices.
evals.expected_output: Module with controller, service, DTOs, Prisma queries, and Jest tests following NestJS patterns

assertions.name: module_structure
assertions.description: Proper NestJS module with @Module decorator wiring controller, service, and imports

assertions.name: dto_validation
assertions.description: DTOs use class-validator decorators (IsString, IsNumber, IsOptional)

assertions.name: thin_controller
assertions.description: Controller delegates to service, no business logic in controller

assertions.name: service_with_prisma
assertions.description: Service injects PrismaService and uses Prisma client for CRUD

assertions.name: unit_tests
assertions.description: Jest tests with Test.createTestingModule and mocked PrismaService

assertions.name: error_handling
assertions.description: NotFoundException thrown for missing resources

evals.prompt: Add a custom NestJS guard that checks if the user has a specific role (admin, editor, viewer). The guard should read the role from the JWT payload and compare against a @Roles() decorator on the controller method.
evals.expected_output: Custom RolesGuard, @Roles decorator, and integration with JwtAuthGuard

assertions.name: roles_decorator
assertions.description: Custom @Roles() decorator using SetMetadata

assertions.name: roles_guard
assertions.description: RolesGuard implements CanActivate, reads roles from Reflector

assertions.name: jwt_integration
assertions.description: Works with existing JwtAuthGuard (checks auth before checking roles)

assertions.name: controller_usage
assertions.description: Example showing @Roles('admin') on a controller method

assertions.name: test_coverage
assertions.description: Tests for allowed role, denied role, and missing role

evals.prompt: Implement a NestJS interceptor for request/response logging. Log: method, URL, status code, response time, and user ID (from JWT). Use structured JSON logging. Don't log health check endpoints.
evals.expected_output: NestJS interceptor with structured logging, timing, and health check exclusion

assertions.name: interceptor_class
assertions.description: Implements NestInterceptor with intercept() method

assertions.name: structured_logging
assertions.description: Logs as structured JSON (not console.log strings)

assertions.name: response_timing
assertions.description: Calculates and logs response time using tap() or finalize()

assertions.name: health_check_excluded
assertions.description: Skips logging for /health or /healthz endpoints

assertions.name: user_id_included
assertions.description: Extracts and logs user ID from JWT payload

evals.prompt: Create a NestJS module for sending emails using SendGrid. It should be a reusable module with a configurable API key via ConfigService. Methods: sendWelcomeEmail(user), sendPasswordReset(user, token), sendNotification(user, subject, body). Include templates.
evals.expected_output: Reusable EmailModule with dynamic configuration, SendGrid integration, and template-based sending

assertions.name: dynamic_module
assertions.description: EmailModule uses forRoot() or forRootAsync() for API key configuration

assertions.name: config_service
assertions.description: API key read from ConfigService, not hardcoded

assertions.name: three_methods
assertions.description: sendWelcomeEmail, sendPasswordReset, sendNotification all implemented

assertions.name: templates
assertions.description: Email templates or template strings for each email type

assertions.name: error_handling
assertions.description: SendGrid API failures handled gracefully

evals.prompt: Set up a NestJS exception filter that catches all exceptions and returns a consistent error response format. Include: correlation ID from request header, structured logging, and different handling for HttpException vs unknown errors.
evals.expected_output: Global exception filter with correlation IDs, structured error logging, and consistent response format

assertions.name: catch_all
assertions.description: @Catch() decorator without specific exception type

assertions.name: correlation_id
assertions.description: Reads X-Correlation-ID from request, generates one if missing

assertions.name: http_vs_unknown
assertions.description: Different handling for HttpException (use its status) vs unknown (500)

assertions.name: structured_logging
assertions.description: Logs error with correlationId, path, method, statusCode as structured JSON

assertions.name: consistent_response
assertions.description: Response shape: {statusCode, error, message, correlationId} on every error
